summary: Moved the assembly stub for func_80148B78 out of CLink's fighter assembly file and into Mewtwo's fighter assembly file. The PR body says the function appears to be part of Mewtwo's specials and references a float in the middle of Mewtwo's sda2 block, making its previous location in ftclink.s incorrect.
what_changed: Removed func_80148B78 and its local label lbl_80148C48 from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s, immediately before ftCLink_OnDeath.
Inserted the same func_80148B78 assembly block into asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s after lbl_80148B60 and before the .rodata section.

The moved function body contains calls to func_8000B1CC, func_80022C30, func_802C519C, and func_80088148, uses fighter data offsets including 0x220c, 0x2d4, and 0x5e8, and loads lbl_804D9D6C@sda21(r2).
No C decompilation or symbol renaming was done; this PR is a file-placement correction for an existing raw assembly function.
smart_moves: Used contextual ownership rather than address adjacency alone: the PR body notes the function likely belongs to Mewtwo specials because it loads data from Mewtwo's sda2 block.

Kept the function at its original symbol/address identity, func_80148B78, while changing only the character assembly file that owns it, preserving matching intent and avoiding unnecessary behavior changes.
Moved the entire assembly block including its branch target label lbl_80148C48, preventing dangling local labels or split control flow across fighter files.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume an assembly function belongs to the character file where it currently appears; validate ownership against data references, nearby character-specific constants, action/special context, and sda2/rodata ownership.

For fighter-specific splits, a function that references a character-specific sda2 block may need to live with that character even if it is located near another character's named functions in the current file layout.
When correcting file ownership for unmatched asm, move the whole contiguous function including epilogue labels and branch labels so future decomp work has clean per-character source boundaries.
Small file-organization fixes can matter for later decompilation because they place unknown functions near the correct fighter's data and specials code.
naming_conventions: CLink fighter assembly file path: asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s.
Mewtwo fighter assembly file path: asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s.
Existing unknown function name func_80148B78 was preserved.
Existing local label lbl_80148C48 was preserved.
Existing named CLink lifecycle function ftCLink_OnDeath remains in ftclink.s after the moved block was removed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Relocate raw assembly by exact address/symbol block when ownership is wrong, without rewriting instructions.
Use sda2 references such as lbl_804D9D6C@sda21(r2) as evidence for which fighter data block a function belongs to.
Keep call targets and instruction bytes unchanged when a PR is only correcting file placement.

The diff excerpt shows a likely comment-address typo on the added func_8000B1CC call line in ftmewtwo.s: the address comment reads 80149BCC while the surrounding sequence and removed version use 80148BCC. There is no evidence in the PR context that this was discussed or intentional.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that decompile or rename func_80148B78 now that it is placed in ftmewtwo.s.
Search for other fighter asm functions whose file ownership may be wrong based on character-specific sda2 or rodata references.
Verify whether the 80149BCC address comment typo in ftmewtwo.s was later corrected.
